summary: A color-graded Police Week ceremony video, posted by the DHS official, set to a music bed with spoken voiceover. Footage shows a uniformed honor guard in dark dress uniforms with white gloves standing at attention on a stage beside a US flag and a Department of Homeland Security flag, a bagpiper in a kilt and dark tunic playing as he walks through a seated audience, and an honor-guard member in a campaign hat laying long-stemmed red roses on a draped table while another stands by. Burned-in white captions track the spoken tribute to ICE and DHS law enforcement. The audio is spoken word over music.

script.scene: Three honor-guard members in dark dress uniforms with white gloves stand at attention on a stage; a US flag and a DHS flag flank a screen behind them. Caption: 'AND YOU DO HAVE A LOVE'.
A bagpiper in a dark tunic and tartan kilt plays the bagpipes while walking through an indoor audience, other kilted players around him. Caption: 'TO HEAR THAT IT'S ICE'.
An honor-guard member in a campaign hat and white gloves bends to place long-stemmed red roses on a black-draped table; a seated man watches at left and another guard stands at right.

notable_text: AND YOU DO HAVE A LOVE; TO HEAR THAT IT'S ICE
